steps.title: Set up the mixture equation
steps.content:
The mixture contains only vitamin D and calcium. Since these are the only two components, their masses must sum to the total mass:

$$\text{vitamin D mass} + \text{calcium mass} = \text{total mass}$$

steps.title: Solve for calcium mass
steps.content:
Substitute the known values into the equation:

$$50 + \text{calcium mass} = 150$$

Subtract 50 from both sides:

$$\text{calcium mass} = 150 - 50 = 100$$

That matches choice C — C is correct.
